detaileddescription.para: UI string data format
Create an Builder handle; this can be initialised with Builder::New() Calling member functions with an uninitialised handle is not allowed.
This is non-virtual since derived Handle types must not contain data or virtual methods.

Loads a string representation of an actor tree into memory. The Actor is not automatically added to the stage. This function will raise an exception for parse and logical structure errors.
The Builder has been initialized.
Preconditions have been met for creating dali objects ie Images, Actors etc
data
A string represenation of an Actor tree
format
The string representation format ie JSON

briefdescription.para: Adds user defined constants to all future style template or animation expansions.

detaileddescription.para: The following shows a list of constants available by default:

entry.para: The Image Directory used by UI.
The Sound Directory used by UI.
The Style directory that the UI uses.
The directory that stores all the images used by UI's style.

simplesect.para: The Builder has been initialized.

parameterdescription.para: The user defined constants used in template expansions.

briefdescription.para: Adds or modifies a user defined constant to all future style template or animation expansions.

detaileddescription.para: e.g. builder.AddConstant("IMAGE_DIRECTORY","/usr/share/images");

simplesect.para: AddConstants(const Property::Map&) for builder pre-defined default constants.
The Builder has been initialized.

parameterdescription.para: The constant name to add or update
The new value for the constant.

simplesect.para: The Builder has been initialized.
A reference to the currently defined configurations.

detaileddescription.para: e.g. Property::Mapmap=builder.GetConstants();//getcopyofcurrentconstants map["IMAGE_DIRECTORY"]="/usr/share/images";//makemodification builder.AddConstants(map);//writebackchanges

simplesect.para: The Builder has been initialized.
A reference to the currently defined constants.

briefdescription.para: Gets a currently defined constant, or returns Property::INVALID.

detaileddescription.para: e.g. Property::Mapmap=builder.GetConstants();//getcopyofcurrentconstants map["IMAGE_DIRECTORY"]="/usr/share/images";//makemodification builder.AddConstants(map);//writebackchanges

simplesect.para: The Builder has been initialized.

parameterdescription.para: The constant name to search for.

detaileddescription.para: Creates an animation from the set of known animations e.g. Animation a = builder.CreateAnimation( "wobble");

simplesect.para: The Builder has been initialized.
Preconditions have been met for creating dali objects ie Images, Actors etc
The animationName exists in the animations section of the data representation
The base handle of the created object

briefdescription.para: Creates an animation from the set of known animations with user defined constants.

detaileddescription.para: e.g. Property::Map map; map["ACTOR"] = actor.GetProperty< std::string >( Dali::Actor::Property::NAME ); // replaces '{ACTOR} in the template Animation a = builder.CreateAnimation( "wobble");

simplesect.para: The Builder has been initialized.
Preconditions have been met for creating dali objects ie Images, Actors etc
The animationName exists in the animations section of the data representation
The map contains all the constant expansions in the style template

parameterdescription.para: The user defined constants used in style template expansion.

simplesect.para: The base handle of the created object

briefdescription.para: Creates an animation from the set of known animations.

detaileddescription.para: The animation is applied to a specific actor. e.g. Actor myInstance = builder.Create( "templateActorTree" ) Animation a = builder.CreateAnimation( "wobble", myInstance );

simplesect.para: The Builder has been initialized.
Preconditions have been met for creating dali objects ie Images, Actors etc
The animationName exists in the animations section of the data representation

parameterdescription.para: The starting point in an actor tree, from which to look for property owners

simplesect.para: The base handle of the created object

briefdescription.para: Creates an animation from the set of known animations with user defined constants.

detaileddescription.para: The animation is applied to a specific actor. e.g. Property::Map map; map["ACTOR"] = actor.GetProperty< std::string >( Dali::Actor::Property::NAME ); // replaces '{ACTOR} in the template Actor myInstance = builder.Create( "templateActorTree" ) Animation a = builder.CreateAnimation( "wobble", myInstance);

simplesect.para: The Builder has been initialized.
Preconditions have been met for creating dali objects ie Images, Actors etc
The animationName exists in the animations section of the data representation
The map contains all the constant expansions in the style template

parameterdescription.para: The user defined constants used in style template expansion.
The starting point in an actor tree, from which to look for property owners

simplesect.para: The base handle of the created object

briefdescription.para: Creates an object (e.g. an actor) from the set of known style templates.

detaileddescription.para: e.g. mActor.Add( Actor::DownCast(builder.Create( "defaultText")) );

simplesect.para: The Builder has been initialized.
Preconditions have been met for creating dali objects ie Images, Actors etc
The templateName exists in the templates section of the data representation and contains 'type' property used to create the object.

parameterdescription.para: The template to apply in creation.

simplesect.para: The base handle of the created object

briefdescription.para: Creates an object from the style templates with user defined constants.

detaileddescription.para: e.g. Property::Map map; map["IMAGE_DIR"] = "/usr/share/images"; // replaces '{IMAGE_DIR} in the template mActor.Add( Actor::DownCast(builder.Create( "defaultImage", map) ) );

simplesect.para: The Builder has been initialized.
Preconditions have been met for creating dali objects ie Images, Actors etc
The templateName exists in the templates section of the data representation and contains 'type' property used to create the object.

parameterdescription.para: The template used to create the object.
The user defined constants used in template expansion.

simplesect.para: The base handle of the created object

briefdescription.para: Creates an object (e.g. an actor) from given json snippet.

detaileddescription.para: e.g. Actor a = Actor::DownCast(builder.CreateFromJson( "{\"type\":\"TextActor\"}"));

simplesect.para: The Builder has been initialized.
Preconditions have been met for creating dali objects ie Images, Actors etc

parameterdescription.para: The json snippet used to create the object.

simplesect.para: The base handle of the created object if any

detaileddescription.para:
Apply a style (a collection of properties) to an actor.
The Builder has been initialized.
Preconditions have been met for creating dali objects ie Images, Actors etc
styleName
The name of the set of style properties to set on the handle object.
handle
Then handle of the object on which to set the properties.
Return true if the style was found

Apply a style (a collection of properties) to an actor from the given json snippet
The Builder has been initialized.
Preconditions have been met for creating dali objects ie Images, Actors etc
handle
Then handle of the object on which to set the properties.
json
The json snippet used to create the object.
Return true if the json snippet was parsed

Add the actor tree in the "stage" section to the actor toActor. ie if the representation has a 'stage' section that contains a tree of actors then builder.AddActors( Stage::GetCurrent().GetRootLayer() ); will create and add the actors to the stage root layer.
toActor
The actor to add the created actors to

Adds actors in the sectionName to the actor toActor. ie if the representation has a sectionName section that contains a tree of actors then builder.AddActors( sectionName, Stage::GetCurrent().GetRootLayer() ); will create and add the actors to the stage root layer.
sectionName
The section name to search for the actor tree
toActor
The actor to add the created actors to

Create a render task set.
The Builder has been initialized.
name
The library name of the render task set.

Get or create Path from the Path instance library. An empty handle is returned otherwise.
The Builder has been initialized.
name
The name of a Path in the loaded representation
A handle to a Path if found, otherwise empty
Get or create a PathConstrainer from the set of known PathConstrainers e.g. PathConstrainer a = builder.GetPathConstrainer( "myPathConstrainer");

simplesect.para: The Builder has been initialized.
The pathConstrainerName exists in the Constrainers section of the data representation
A handle to a PathConstrainer if found, otherwise empty

detaileddescription.para: Get or create a LinearConstrainer from the set of known LinearConstrainers e.g. LinearConstrainer a = builder.GetLinearConstrainer( "myLinearConstrainer");

simplesect.para: The Builder has been initialized.
The linearConstrainerName exists in the Constrainers section of the data representation
A handle to a LinearConstrainer if found, otherwise empty

briefdescription.para: Signal emitted when a quit action is requested by the builder.

detaileddescription.para:
Creates an Builder object.
A handle to the Builder control.
This class provides the ability to load and style an actor tree from a string representation.
The following is an example in JSON.
